entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species being studied for its response to drought stress.

entities.name: MID-SEASON DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Environmental stress occurring during the mid-season growth phase of the crop.

entities.name: DROUGHT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the plant lines to withstand mid-season drought stress.

entities.name: LEAF-ROLLING
entities.description: A physiological response where leaves roll to reduce the effective area of the uppermost leaves.

entities.name: LEAF DISSICATION
entities.description: The drying out of leaf tissue used as a visual indicator for resistance or susceptibility.

entities.name: VISUAL SELECTION
entities.description: The process of selecting plant lines based on observable physical characteristics like leaf disiccation.

entities.name: LEAF WATER-POTENTIAL
entities.description: The measure of the energy state of water in the leaf tissue.

entities.name: LIGHT-EXTINCTION COEFFICIENT K
entities.description: A coefficient indicating the rate of light absorption/extinction in the canopy.

entities.name: LEAF TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature of the leaf surface.

entities.name: RADIATION SHEDDING
entities.description: The process of reducing heat load by reflecting or transmitting solar radiation.

entities.name: LEAF CONDUCTANCE
entities.description: The measure of the ease with which water vapor exits the leaf.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION RATE
entities.description: The rate at which water is lost from the leaf surface per unit area.

entities.name: LEAF SURFACE MICROCLIMATE
entities.description: The immediate atmospheric environment surrounding the leaf surface.

entities.name: WATER LOSS
entities.description: The loss of water from the plant tissue.

entities.name: GROWTH
entities.description: The biological process of increasing in size or biomass.
